title: Alternate Name for RoE NTV NME
display_title: Alternate Name for RoE NTV NME
wikitext:
[]: Alternate Name for RoE/NTV/NME             Quote Post by Aether » Mon Nov 10, 2014 5:16 pm So, who thinks instead of calling the new movies "Rebuild of Evangelion" or "Evangelion: New Theatrical Version", it should have been called "Argon Exodus Evangelion"? Or maybe name each one something different? So it goes like "Argon Exodus Evangelion" "Xenon Leviticus Evangelion" "Krypton Numbers Evangelion" and call the final one: "Radon Deuteronomy Evangelion"? Huh? Anyone? No? No one? Just me? The Jewish chemistry-geek? Ok. . . .

[]: Re: Alternate Name for RoE/NTV/NME             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Nov 10, 2014 5:24 pm Aether wrote: "Radon Deuteronomy Evangelion"

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Mon Nov 10, 2014 6:03 pm ^ ...dude, that's brilliant.

[]: Re: Alternate Name for RoE/NTV/NME             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 10, 2014 7:39 pm Aether wrote: "Radon Deuteronomy Evangelion"?

[]:             Quote Post by strawhatpatriot » Fri Nov 14, 2014 10:09 pm Well the "Genesis" in the title is supposed to refer to the word "beginning" or "birth". It's not just used as a random insert bible name. And the Neon means "New", that way Neon Genesis Evangelion can translate into "New Beginning Gospel", or as in the manner of the Japanese title Shin Seiki Evangelion, "New Century Gospel". So some possible alternatives for the name could be something like: Neos Origins Credos Nova Genos Evangeline Newo Bher Evangile

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Nov 14, 2014 11:32 pm Does Neon actually mean new? I thought the Westernized title was devised to capture the the spirit of the original by using vaguely foreign words to 'sound complicated'. New Genesis Evangelion actually makes sense.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Fri Nov 14, 2014 11:55 pm I thought it was 'Neon' because...future

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Nov 15, 2014 12:19 am I'm just gonna use Occam's Razor on this and say that Anno just chose it because it sounded cool/complicated...just like he did with Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Viethra » Sat Nov 22, 2014 6:56 am Guy Nacks wrote: I'm just gonna use Occam's Razor on this and say that Anno just chose it because it sounded cool/complicated...just like he did with Evangelion.

Or maybe name each one something different? So it goes like "Argon Exodus Evangelion" "Xenon Leviticus Evangelion" "Krypton Nu
